gene_description: Protein Phosphatase Mg2+/Mn2+ Dependent 1K — mitochondrial phosphatase that activates BCKDH (branched-chain alpha-keto acid dehydrogenase), the rate-limiting enzyme in branched-chain amino acid (BCAA) catabolism. Variants affect circulating BCAA levels and insulin secretion.

source.title: Effect of the interaction between diet composition and the PPM1K genetic variant on insulin resistance and β cell function markers during weight loss
source.finding: PPM1K rs1440581 T allele is associated with elevated serum valine and total BCAAs. Significant gene-diet interactions: T allele carriers show higher insulin reduction on high-fat diet but smaller glucose improvement during weight-loss. CC genotype associated with cardiovascular disease risk.

CC.effect: Homozygous C carriers show elevated serum valine and total BCAA levels. PPM1K phosphatase activity may be altered, affecting BCKDH-mediated BCAA catabolism. Associated with increased cardiovascular disease risk. Decreased PPM1K expression impairs glucose-stimulated insulin secretion in pancreatic beta cells.
CC.recommendation: Monitor cardiovascular risk markers. Moderate protein intake with balanced BCAA sources. Consider lower BCAA supplementation. Regular fasting glucose and insulin monitoring. Low-fat dietary pattern may be preferable.

CT.effect: Heterozygous carriers show intermediate BCAA levels and mixed metabolic response to dietary interventions.
CT.recommendation: Balanced macronutrient intake. Standard metabolic monitoring.

TC.effect: Heterozygous carriers show intermediate BCAA levels and mixed metabolic response to dietary interventions.
TC.recommendation: Balanced macronutrient intake. Standard metabolic monitoring.

TT.effect: T allele carriers show better glucose improvement during weight-loss interventions. Higher insulin reduction on high-fat diets (gene-diet interaction). Normal BCAA catabolism.
TT.recommendation: Standard dietary guidelines. Responds well to weight-loss dietary interventions.
